articleTitle: The effectiveness of digital health interventions on anthropometric and healthy behavior in women with polycystic ovarian syndrome: a systematic review with meta-analysis.
takeaways: Digital health interventions showed statistically significant improvements in body weight, BMI, and waist circumference measurements in women with PCOS compared to control groups
App-based dietary tracking and exercise monitoring programs demonstrated superior effectiveness compared to standard care alone for managing PCOS-related weight concerns
Women using digital health platforms showed improved adherence to lifestyle modification recommendations, with intervention groups maintaining higher compliance rates throughout study periods
Digital interventions incorporating both nutrition and physical activity components produced greater anthropometric improvements than single-component programs
Mobile health applications with healthcare provider integration showed enhanced effectiveness for sustained behavior change in PCOS management compared to standalone apps
